title: Science, Mathematics and Research for Transformation
description: The Science, Mathematics and Research for Transformation (SMART) Scholarship for Service Program has been established by the Department of Defense (DoD) to support undergraduate and graduate students pursuing degrees in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) disciplines.
text:
Submission Date
Specific submission dates may be found at https://www.smartscholarship.org.
Program Description
The Science, Mathematics and Research for Transformation (SMART) Scholarship for Service Program has been established by the Department of Defense (DoD) to support undergraduate and graduate students pursuing degrees in science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) disciplines. The program aims to increase the number of civilian scientists and engineers working at DoD laboratories.
Eligibility Requirements
A U.S. citizen
18 years of age or older
Able to participate in summer internships at DoD laboratories
Willing to accept post-graduate employment with the DoD
A student in good standing with a minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale (as calculated by the SMART application)
Pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree in one of the approved STEM disciplines (view the list and check eligibility via the SMART website)
Conditions
A SMART Program participant may not accept simultaneous remuneration from another major scholarship. Other major awards are defined as: a) funding from any other federal scholarship, fellowship, grant or funding source excluding federally funded student loans and/or salary supplements provided by DoD agencies to DoD retention awardees and, b) Other scholarships, fellowships or grants exceeding $5,000 total per academic year.
